steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to "specify an element's relative abundance on Earth." Specify demands a concrete value, not a general statement about methods or comparisons.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Mass fraction, mole fraction, and volume fraction can all be used to express an element's relative abundance in a given environment.’: Describes three methods of expressing abundance but gives no actual abundance value for any element. Does not specify anything. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Expressed as a mass fraction, the relative abundance of potassium on Earth is 160 ppm, or 0.016%.’: States a specific element (potassium) and its exact relative abundance (160 ppm / 0.016%) drawn directly from the notes. This is the only choice that specifies a concrete value. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The relative abundance of the element potassium on Earth can be expressed in three different ways.’: Names the element but still gives no actual abundance figure. Saying it "can be expressed" is not specifying it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘On Earth, the mass fraction of potassium is greater than that of calcium.’: Factually wrong—potassium is 160 ppm while calcium is 17,100 ppm, so calcium is far more abundant. Also a comparison, not a specification. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
